channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Apparent swatting call at Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene's Georgia home leads to deadly car accident
item.description: An apparent swatting call at the home of Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene, R-Ga., led to a car accident during the police response, which left one woman dead and an officer injured.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican-led states are rolling out plans that could aid Trump's mass deportation effort
item.description: Governors and lawmakers in Republican-led states are rolling out plans that could aid President-elect Donald Trump's effort to deport millions of people living in the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Elon Musk warns Republicans against standing in Trump's way — or his
item.description: Donald Trump’s second term comes with the specter of the world’s richest man serving as his political enforcer.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Juliet Linderman And Martha Mendoza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democrats frustrated with Biden's 'lame duck' end to presidency
item.description: With 42 days until President Joe Biden cedes power, some Democrats are exasperated at how he is spending his final days in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: US indictment accuses two Syrian officials of torture at notorious prison
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. prosecutors are accusing two senior Syrian officials of overseeing a notorious prison that tortured peaceful protesters and other political prisoners, including a 26-year-old American woman who was later believed to have been executed.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down as RNC co-chair and addressing speculation about Florida Senate seat
item.description: Lara Trump will step down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee as she considers a number of potential options with her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, set to return to the White House. Among those possibilities is replacing Florida Sen.
item.dc:creator: JULIET LINDERMAN and MARTHA MENDOZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard, Trump's pick for intel chief, faces questions on Capitol Hill amid Syria fallout
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for intelligence chief, Tulsi Gabbard, is making her case on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Farnoush Amiri, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After second meeting with Hegseth, Ernst hints at whether she will or won't support confirmation
item.description: Republican Sen. Joni Ernst, a member of the Armed Services Committee, signaled toward supporting Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump's embattled nominee for defense secretary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New California Sen. Adam Schiff wants to be more than a Trump antagonist
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrat Adam Schiff stood on the Senate floor almost five years ago as a House impeachment manager and made a passionate case that Donald Trump should be removed from office for abusing the power of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US hostage envoy in Beirut to seek information on missing journalist Austin Tice
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government's top hostage negotiator is in Beirut in hopes of collecting information on the whereabouts of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for 12 years, the State Department said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US says terror designation doesn't bar talks with Syrian rebel group
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department said Monday it is not actively reviewing the “foreign terrorist organization” designation of the main Syrian rebel group that overthrew Bashar Assad’s government this weekend.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Heritage report offers roadmap to defeat Tren de Aragua, the mega-gang taking over US cities
item.description: New report a starting point to understand the Tren de Aragua organization that was “honed and perfected inside Venezuelan prison walls then exported throughout the Western Hemisphere.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Manchin, Welch unveil bill placing term limits on Supreme Court
item.description: West Virginia Sen. Joe Manchin (I) and Vermont Sen. Peter Welch (D) have introduced a resolution to impose 18-year term limits on Supreme Court justices, which would require some turnover on the high court every two years.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court turns away challenge to Hawaii handgun license law
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to take up a defendant’s Second Amendment challenge to Hawaii laws that make it a crime to carry handguns or ammunition in public without a license.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's 'Meet the Press' interview
item.description: President-elect Trump's first sit-down interview since winning a second White House term aired Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump 'can't guarantee' tariffs won't raise prices for consumers
item.description: "I can't guarantee anything. I can't guarantee tomorrow. But I can say that if you look at my — just pre-COVID, we had the greatest economy in the history of our country," Trump responded.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Russia abandoned Syria's Assad, never should have been involved
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said on Sunday that Russia's abandonment of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad led to his downfall.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP to choose chairs for influential committees: Who’s in the running
item.description: House Republican leaders will choose new chairs for a number of influential committees this week, people who will have the power to help deliver on President-elect Trump’s priorities in the GOP trifecta over the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massachusetts Democrat says she was targeted by bomb threat
item.description: Rep. Lori Tahan, D-Mass., shared that she and her family were the target of a bomb threat on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the billionaires stocking Trump’s next administration
item.description: President-elect Trump has named several billionaires to top roles in his next administration — a combination worth over $360 billion and that includes the world’s richest person.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Congressman Torres blasts crime in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The cold-blooded shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in the heart of Manhattan shocked New Yorkers, including Congressman Ritchie Torres, leaving the lawmaker to pose the question: “What is happening in New York City?” Torres joined PIX on Politics to discuss city crim
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk funded a super PAC comparing Trump’s position on abortion to Ruth Bader Ginsburg’s
item.description: Elon Musk was the sole funder of a super PAC that focused on advertising likening Donald Trump’s stance on abortion to that of late Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Talking Sense: Coming together to solve tough problems
item.description: Mariah Levison and Rob Fersh help people find consensus on how to solve divisive issues, ranging from prison reform to what art should hang in the Minnesota Capitol. Learn how they do it in this MPR News Talking Sense conversation hosted by MPR News correspondent Catharine Richert.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis, Catharine Richert, Maja Beckstrom, and Annie Baxter, MPR News

item.title: Steven Horsford to hand off role as chair of Congressional Black Caucus
item.description: Nevada Congressman Steven Horsford will no longer be the chair of the Congressional Black Caucus for the 119th Congress.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, KLAS, CBS News
